meaning: n.宸ヤ綔娓呴棽鑰屾姤閰赴鍘氱殑鑱屼綅,鎸傚悕鑱屽姟,闂茶亴
examples: She found him an exalted sinecure as a Fellow of the Library of Congress.
This is hardly a [not a, no] sinecure.
There are uncomfortable lessons here. First, success is not a sinecure.
But finding this green in two does not give you a two-putt sinecure for par.
New York's powerful Irish Catholics are unlikely to give up what they regard as a sinecure without a fierce struggle.
